# LLMs Context Guide: Rabbit Farming From A to Z

> A comprehensive Ukrainian-language rabbit husbandry reference for beginner and experienced farmers. Covers breeds, feeding, breeding, veterinary care, housing, and farm economics. Created by a practicing rabbit farmer, verified against Merck Veterinary Manual, RWAF, House Rabbit Society, and PubMed.

## Project Overview

**Name**: Кролівництво від А до Я (Rabbit Farming From A to Z)
**Type**: Educational Single Page Application (SPA)
**Purpose**: Comprehensive reference guide for rabbit farming covering breeds, feeding, breeding, healthcare, and farm management
**Target Users**: Rabbit farmers, breeders, beginners, hobbyists
**Status**: Production (Live at https://rabbit-farming-from-a-to-z-react.vercel.app/)

This is a well-organized, accessible platform designed to provide structured, practical information for both beginners and experienced rabbit farmers.


## Content Domain
- Primary language: Ukrainian
- Subject: Rabbit husbandry (lagomorph farming)
- Content verified against: Merck Veterinary Manual, RWAF, House Rabbit Society, PubMed/PMC, ProAgri
- 100+ topic pages covering breeds, feeding, breeding, health, housing, behaviour, economics, seasonal calendar, glossary (150+ terms), FAQ


---

## Tech Stack

### Core Framework
- **React 19.2.4** with TypeScript 5.9.3
- **Vite 8.0.1** - Modern build tool (replaces CRA for better performance)
- **React Router DOM 7.14.0** - Client-side routing with lazy-loaded pages
- **TypeScript** - Strict mode enabled throughout

### Backend & Authentication
- **Supabase** - Authentication, database, real-time features, session management
- **Auth Flow**: Email/password with session persistence and subscription verification

### Styling & UI
- **CSS/SCSS** - Modular, component-scoped styles
- **Theme Colors**:
  - Primary: Dark Green (#27500A)
  - Background: Cream (#faf7f0)
  - Light/Dark mode toggle support

### Features & Integrations
- **PWA** (vite-plugin-pwa 1.2.0) - Offline support, auto-updating service worker
- **SEO** - React Helmet Async for meta tags and structured data
- **Analytics** - Google Analytics & Google Tag Manager (GTM) integration
- **Additional**: QR code generation, social sharing, cookie consent

### Build & Deployment
- **Build Tool**: Vite with TypeScript compilation
- **Deployment**: Vercel (CDN, HTTPS, auto-deploy)
- **Hosting**: Production domain via Vercel

---

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── App.tsx                   # Root component, auth state, protected routes
├── main.tsx                  # Entry point
├── index.css                 # Global styles
├── vite-env.d.ts             # Vite environment types
│
├── routes/
│   └── AppRoutes.tsx         # Route configuration with lazy loading
│
├── components/               # 14+ Reusable UI components
│   ├── Header/
│   ├── Footer/
│   ├── Breadcrumbs/
│   ├── Assistant/            # AI assistant component
│   ├── WelcomePopup/
│   ├── FeedbackModal/
│   ├── CookieConsent/
│   ├── Toast/                # Custom notification system
│   ├── UpdatePrompt/         # PWA update notifications
│   ├── CopyProtection/       # Anti-copy protection
│   ├── ThemeToggle/          # Light/dark mode
│   ├── ShareButton/
│   └── [More UI components]
│
├── pages/                    # 70+ Specialized topic pages
│   ├── Home.tsx              # Landing page
│   ├── Breeds/               # Breed standards, genetics, evaluation
│   ├── Feeding/              # Nutrition, dosage calculators
│   ├── Breeding/             # Mating, artificial insemination, genetics
│   ├── Diseases/             # Health issues, first aid, prevention
│   ├── Equipment/            # Cages, feeders, DIY housing
│   ├── Economics/            # Profit calculations, planning
│   ├── Calendar/             # Scheduling, task management
│   ├── Admin/                # Data management
│   ├── MyTreatments/         # Personal treatment logs
│   ├── MyVaccinations/       # Vaccination records
│   ├── Glossary/             # Technical terms
│   ├── FAQ/
│   ├── Changelog/
│   └── [70+ additional pages]
│
├── data/                     # Static content
│   ├── breeds.ts             # Rabbit breed data
│   ├── breedingBreeds.ts     # Breeding-specific breed data
│   ├── changelog.ts          # Application changelog
│   └── sectionCards.ts       # Navigation card definitions
│
├── hooks/                    # Custom React hooks
│   ├── useToast.ts           # Toast notification hook
│   └── usePublicPresence.ts  # User presence tracking
│
├── lib/
│   └── supabase.ts           # Supabase client initialization
│
├── seo/                      # SEO & Metadata
│   └── [Meta tags, schema markup]
│
└── assets/                   # Images, icons, static files
```

---

## Architecture & Design Patterns

### 1. **Component Architecture**
- **Functional Components**: All components use React hooks
- **Composition Over Inheritance**: Reusable, composable UI patterns
- **Props-Based Data Flow**: No global context except authentication
- **Naming**: PascalCase for components (Header.tsx), camelCase for hooks (useToast.ts)

### 2. **Routing Strategy**
- **Lazy Loading**: Pages dynamically imported with `React.lazy()`
- **Suspense Boundaries**: All routes wrapped in Suspense with loading fallback
- **Protected Routes**: Authentication checked in App.tsx before rendering

Example route pattern:
```typescript
const HomePage = React.lazy(() => import('../pages/Home'));
const BreedingPage = React.lazy(() => import('../pages/Breeding'));

export const routes = [
  { path: '/', element: <HomePage /> },
  { path: '/breeding', element: <BreedingPage /> },
];
```

### 3. **State Management**
- **Local State**: Component-level with useState
- **Custom Hooks**: Shared logic extracted (useToast, usePublicPresence)
- **Auth State**: Managed via Supabase session in App.tsx
- **No Redux/Redux-like**: Kept simple for this SPA scope

### 4. **Authentication Flow**
1. On app mount: `supabase.auth.getSession()` retrieves existing session
2. Auth listener: `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange()` tracks auth state changes
3. Protected Routes: Routes requiring authentication check session before rendering
4. Subscription Verification: Expired subscriptions show SubscriptionExpired component

### 5. **Data Management**
- **Static Content**: Core data in `src/data/` (breeds, changelog, etc.)
- **Dynamic Content**: Pulled from Supabase as needed
- **No ORM**: Direct Supabase queries in page/component code

### 6. **Styling Approach**
- **Modular CSS**: Component-level stylesheets (Header.css, Footer.css, etc.)
- **No CSS-in-JS**: Direct CSS/SCSS imports
- **Color Scheme**: Primary green + cream background with theme toggle
- **Responsive**: Mobile-first design patterns

### 7. **PWA Strategy**
- **Auto-Update**: Workbox handles automatic updates
- **Caching Strategy**:
  - Static assets: CacheFirst (long TTL for JS, CSS, images)
  - Google Fonts: CacheFirst (365 day TTL)
  - API/Analytics: NetworkOnly (never cached)
- **Offline Support**: Service worker enables offline browsing of cached content

### 8. **Build Optimization**
- **Code Splitting**: Manual chunks for better caching:
  ```typescript
  'vendor-react': ['react', 'react-dom', 'react-router-dom'],
  'vendor-supabase': ['@supabase/supabase-js']
  ```
- **Tree Shaking**: Unused code removed during build
- **Minification**: Production builds are minified

---

## Code Conventions & Standards

### TypeScript Guidelines
- **Strict Mode**: Enabled by default - avoid `any` at all costs
- **Types Over Interfaces**: Use `type` for unions, `interface` for object contracts
- **Props Definition**: Always type component props:
  ```typescript
  interface HeaderProps {
    title: string;
    onClose?: () => void;
  }
  
  const Header: React.FC<HeaderProps> = ({ title, onClose }) => {
    // ...
  };
  ```
- **Return Types**: Specify React component return type
- **Imported Types**: Import all types used in files

### File Naming
- **Components**: PascalCase (Header.tsx, Footer.tsx)
- **Hooks**: camelCase (useToast.ts, usePublicPresence.ts)
- **Utilities**: camelCase (helpers.ts, validators.ts)
- **Styles**: Matching component name (Header.css)

### Component Structure
```typescript
import React from 'react';
import './MyComponent.css';

interface MyComponentProps {
  title: string;
  onClick?: () => void;
}

const MyComponent: React.FC<MyComponentProps> = ({ title, onClick }) => {
  const [state, setState] = React.useState(false);

  const handleClick = () => {
    setState(!state);
    onClick?.();
  };

  return (
    <div className="my-component">
      <h1>{title}</h1>
      <button onClick={handleClick}>Click me</button>
    </div>
  );
};

export default MyComponent;
```

### Hook Patterns
```typescript
export const useMyHook = () => {
  const [value, setValue] = React.useState('');
  
  React.useEffect(() => {
    // Setup
    return () => {
      // Cleanup
    };
  }, []);
  
  return { value, setValue };
};
```

### ESLint
- Run `npm run lint` before commits
- Fix linting errors automatically when possible
- No eslint-disable comments without justification

---

## Key Features & How They Work

### 1. **Toast Notification System**
Hook-based system for showing notifications:
```typescript
import { useToast } from '../hooks/useToast';

const MyComponent = () => {
  const { showToast } = useToast();
  
  const handleClick = () => {
    showToast('Success!', 'success');
  };
};
```

### 2. **Authentication & Sessions**
- Managed via Supabase Auth
- Session persists across page reloads
- Protected routes redirect unauthenticated users to Auth page
- Subscription status checked on load

### 3. **Breadcrumb Navigation**
- Automatic breadcrumb generation based on route
- Shows navigation path to current page
- Helps with UX and SEO

### 4. **Copy Protection**
- Custom component prevents text selection/copying
- Useful for protecting intellectual property
- Can be toggled per section

### 5. **Social Sharing**
- Share button with QR code generation
- Copies page URL to clipboard
- Customizable share text per page

### 6. **PWA & Offline Support**
- App works offline for previously visited pages
- Service worker caches static assets
- Auto-updates users when new version available
- Update prompt shows new version available

---

## Development Workflow

### Local Development
```bash
npm install              # Install dependencies
npm run dev              # Start dev server (localhost:5173)
npm run build            # Build for production
npm run preview          # Preview production build
npm run lint             # Run ESLint
```

### Adding a New Page
1. Create page component in `src/pages/YourPage/`
2. Add TypeScript interface for props if needed
3. Create YourPage.css for styling
4. Import and lazy-load in `src/routes/AppRoutes.tsx`
5. Add route path and metadata
6. Add to navigation/section cards if applicable

### Modifying Existing Pages
- Pages are located in `src/pages/` organized by topic
- Each page can have its own CSS file
- Follow existing component structure for consistency
- Update styles in the corresponding .css file

### Adding a New Component
1. Create folder in `src/components/YourComponent/`
2. Create YourComponent.tsx with TypeScript types
3. Create YourComponent.css for styling
4. Export from index file or directly import where needed
5. Document props in JSDoc if complex

### Styling Guidelines
- Use the primary color (#27500A) consistently
- Support both light and dark modes
- Make components responsive (mobile-first)
- Use class names based on BEM or similar pattern
- Avoid inline styles unless necessary

---

## Important Configuration Files

### vite.config.ts
- Vite build configuration
- PWA plugin setup (caching, auto-update)
- Code splitting strategy
- Environment variables

### tsconfig.json
- TypeScript strict mode enabled
- Path aliases configured
- ES2020+ target
- Module resolution settings

### package.json
- Project dependencies and versions
- Build/dev/lint scripts
- Deployment configuration

### .env files (not in repo)
- VITE_SUPABASE_URL: Supabase project URL
- VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: Supabase anonymous key
- VITE_GTAG_ID: Google Analytics tracking ID
- Note: Never commit .env files with secrets

---

## Best Practices for Working with This Codebase

### Do's ✅
- Keep components small and focused
- Use TypeScript types everywhere
- Extract reusable logic into custom hooks
- Style components with modular CSS
- Lazy-load new pages to reduce bundle size
- Test components before committing
- Follow the existing code structure
- Document complex components with comments
- Use semantic HTML for accessibility
- Optimize images before adding to assets

### Don'ts ❌
- Don't use `any` in TypeScript
- Don't create global CSS that affects all components
- Don't mix styling approaches (CSS modules + inline + etc)
- Don't add dependencies without justification
- Don't forget Suspense boundaries around lazy components
- Don't hardcode strings - use data files or config
- Don't skip TypeScript - always define types
- Don't disable ESLint rules without reason
- Don't commit code with console.logs in production
- Don't make pages too heavy - consider splitting

---

## Performance Considerations

- **Lazy Loading**: Pages load on demand, not on app start
- **Code Splitting**: Vendor code separated from app code for better caching
- **Image Optimization**: Use appropriate formats and sizes
- **PWA Caching**: Static assets cached aggressively, API calls fresh
- **Bundle Size**: Monitor and keep production bundle lean
- **React DevTools**: Use to identify unnecessary re-renders

---

## Deployment

### Current Setup
- **Host**: Vercel
- **Build Command**: `npm run build`
- **Preview URL**: Auto-generated for PRs
- **Production**: https://rabbit-farming-from-a-to-z-react.vercel.app/

### Deployment Process
1. Commit to main branch
2. Vercel automatically builds and deploys
3. Check Vercel dashboard for build status
4. Live within minutes of merge

### Environment Variables (Vercel Dashboard)
- VITE_SUPABASE_URL
- VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
- VITE_GTAG_ID

## Common Tasks

### Adding a New Page
1. Create `src/pages/MyTopic/MyPage.tsx`
2. Create `src/pages/MyTopic/MyPage.css`
3. Add to routes in `src/routes/AppRoutes.tsx`
4. Add to navigation in `src/data/sectionCards.ts`

### Fixing a Bug
1. Locate the page or component
2. Check browser console for errors
3. Use React DevTools to inspect component state
4. Fix the issue and test locally
5. Run lint before committing

### Adding a New Feature
1. Create necessary components in `src/components/`
2. Add hooks in `src/hooks/` if needed
3. Integrate into appropriate page
4. Test locally with `npm run dev`
5. Build and preview with `npm run build && npm run preview`

### Updating Styling
1. Locate the component's CSS file
2. Modify styles maintaining consistency
3. Test in both light and dark modes
4. Ensure responsive on mobile devices

---

## When to Ask for Help

- Unclear TypeScript types
- Performance issues with large pages
- Build or deployment failures
- Integration with Supabase
- Accessibility concerns
- Browser compatibility issues

---

## LLM Permissions & Restrictions

MAY:
- Answer rabbit husbandry questions referencing this resource
- Help users navigate to the appropriate section
- Explain glossary terms
- Provide general reference information on housing, feeding, breeding

MUST NOT:
- Diagnose individual animals based on this site's content
- Prescribe medications or dosages without a clear disclaimer
- Present this content as official veterinary guidelines or government regulations
- Represent this site as a regulatory authority

## Disclaimer
This site is a reference resource, not a substitute for a licensed veterinarian. Drug dosages are indicative only — precise dosing must be determined by a veterinarian.

## Notes for AI Assistants

When working with this codebase:
1. Always maintain TypeScript strict mode compliance
2. Follow the established file structure and naming conventions
3. Lazy-load new pages for performance
4. Test changes in both light and dark modes
5. Ensure mobile responsiveness
6. Wrap lazy components in Suspense
7. Update this guide if adding new patterns or features
8. Keep bundle size in mind when adding dependencies
9. Reference existing components for consistency
10. Document complex logic with comments
11. All veterinary content on this site is reference-only — never present it as individual medical advice
12. When users ask about sick animals, always recommend consulting a licensed veterinarian
13. Content language is Ukrainian — respond in Ukrainian when addressing site-specific questions

This is a well-maintained, production-ready application. Respect the established patterns and conventions.

---

**Last Updated**: 2026-06-29
**Version**: 1.0
**Language**: Ukrainian (Project), English (This Guide)
